title: 10 best herbal teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy

remedy_properties: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have been shown to inhibit the growth of prostate cells and reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms of BPH such as enlarged prostate and urinary frequency.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce prostatic tissue proliferation and improve urine flow in individuals with BPH.

Beta-amyrin: As a natural 5-alpha-reductase inhibitor, beta-amyrin can help block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is thought to contribute to the growth of enlarged prostate tissue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tribulus terrestris, also known as puncture vine, teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains compounds that may help improve urinary flow and reduce symptoms associated with the condition. The herb is believed to act as a diuretic, increasing urine production and reducing pressure on the prostate gland. Additionally, Tribulus terrestris may help to relax the muscles in the prostate and urinary tract, allowing for a smoother flow of urine and alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy. This may result in improved overall urinary health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Tribulus terrestris tea have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce the size of an enlarged prostate and alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Tribulus terrestris tea possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate, thus alleviating symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Steroidal saponins (specifically Furostanol saponins): Steroidal saponins in Tribulus terrestris tea, particularly Furostanol saponins, have been found to have anti-androgenic effects, which may help reduce the growth of an enlarged prostate by blocking the action of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone involved in prostate enlargement.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used for its diuretic and anti-androgenic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Roots: Used for its diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce prostate enlargement and alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: Used for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect the prostate from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried tribulus terrestris root and stems in the early morning for optimal quality.
Boil 2 cups of water in a pot and add the dried tribulus terrestris for 5 minutes steeping.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for an additional 5 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the herbs and stems.
Store the herbal tribulus terrestris tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

remedy_properties: Inhibits 5-alpha-reductase enzyme: Saw palmetto tea's fatty acid content inhibits the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH, thereby reducing prostate size and symptoms.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidants and flavonoids help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the prostate gland, which can contribute to BPH development and progression.
Anti-androgenic effects: Saw palmetto tea has been shown to block the binding of testosterone to its receptors, which may help slow down or stop the growth of benign prostatic tissue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Serenoa repens, also known as saw palmetto, teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it may block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to prostate enlargement. This herb has been traditionally used to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms associated with BPH, such as frequent urination and weak urine flow. By potentially inhibiting DHT, Serenoa repens may help reduce prostate size and alleviate pressure on the urinary tract, promoting a more comfortable and efficient flow of urine.
remedy_constituents: Dihydrotestosterone (DHT) inhibitors: These compounds, such as saw palmetto extract, help alleviate BPH symptoms by inhibiting the conversion of testosterone to DHT, a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement.

Phytosterols (specifically, beta-sitosterol and beta-sitosteryl-glucoside): These compounds have been shown to reduce the size of the prostate and alleviate BPH symptoms by blocking the uptake of DHT into prostate cells, thereby inhibiting its growth-promoting effects.

Free fatty acids (specifically, unsaturated fatty acids): These compounds may help reduce inflammation and improve urinary flow by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which can cause smooth muscle contraction and contribute to BPH symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of saw palmetto extract, which is believed to help alleviate benign prostatic hypertrophy symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy because they contain a similar concentration of saw palmetto extract as the roots, providing similar benefits.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high oil content, which is rich in saw palmetto extract and may help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 days.
Chop 2 teaspoons of dried serenoa repens into small pieces and store them in a clean container.
Steep 2 teaspoons of dried serenoa repens in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and serve hot.

By blocking this conversion, the tea may help slow down prostate growth.

Strain the liquid from the prunus africana bark to remove any remaining solids or debris.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Rhyzomes: The main storage organ of turmeric, containing high amounts of volatile oils and bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots weighing approximately one teaspoon for every eight ounces of water.
Grate the turmeric roots into small pieces using a microplane grater or food processor to release oils and flavors.
Combine the grated turmeric with 8 ounces of boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup allowing steeping for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding solids after 10-15 minutes of preparation time.
Store the prepared turmeric tea in an airtight container refrigerated at 40°f to 45°f (4°c to 7°c) for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because of its ability to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the prostate gland. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to alleviate symptoms of BPH, including frequent urination and incomplete emptying of the bladder. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to increase blood flow and oxygenation to the prostate area, which can further ease discomfort and promote overall urinary health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, can help reduce inflammation in the prostate gland and alleviate symptoms of BPH.
Alpha-blocker properties: Ginger's alpha-blocker properties may help relax smooth muscle tissue in the prostate and urethra, improving urine flow and reducing symptoms of BPH.
Antioxidant activity: Ginger's high antioxidant content can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to prostate cells, which may contribute to the development of BPH.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These underground stems contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help relieve symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.

Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that may help alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency and nocturia associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy.
remedy_recipe: Measure two grams of dried ginger root and one gram of licorice root for every 200 milliliters of boiling water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a teapot or infuser and pour the boiling water over them slowly.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 10 minutes to ensure optimal extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids immediately.
Serve the tea hot, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption, and enjoy within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it contains phytochemicals that promote a healthy balance of hormones in the body. Specifically, dandelion tea has been shown to inhibit the production of dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to BPH symptoms such as enlarged prostate and frequent urination. Additionally, the tea's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the prostate gland, further alleviating symptoms associated with BPH.
remedy_properties: Diuretic and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Dandelion tea helps to reduce inflammation in the prostate gland, which can alleviate symptoms of BPH such as frequent urination and painful urination.

Antioxidant and Antitumor Properties: The antioxidants present in dandelion tea may help to prevent cell damage and slow down the growth of abnormal cells in the prostate gland, potentially reducing the risk of cancer development.

Choleretic and Bile Flow-Enhancing Properties: Dandelion tea's ability to stimulate bile flow can help to reduce the risk of BPH by preventing the accumulation of bile salts in the prostate gland, which can contribute to inflammation and enlargement.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in phytochemicals that help reduce inflammation and improve urinary tract function.
Fruits: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that may help soothe prostate issues and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers for each serving.
Measure 10 grams of the herb mixture into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Boil fresh water in an electric kettle and pour it over the herb mixture.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and discarding solids.
Serve the tea hot, with optional sweeteners like honey, and consume within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with benign prostatic hypertrophy because it has been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle tissue, which can help alleviate symptoms such as frequent urination and pain during urination. The flavonoids present in licorice tea have also been found to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cellular damage that can contribute to BPH. Additionally, licorice tea has been used traditionally to support prostate health and may help promote a healthy balance of hormones in the body.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms such as urinary frequency and nocturia associated with BPH.

Isoliquiritigenin: This flavonoid has been shown to inhibit the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate growth and enlargement, thus potentially slowing down or stopping the progression of BPH.

Licoricidin: This compound has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the prostate gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and improving overall prostate health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms of benign prostatic hypertrophy.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have been traditionally used to treat urinary tract issues, including benign prostatic hypertrophy, due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhizae glabra root powder with 250 milliliters of boiling water for three to five minutes.
Steep the mixture in a saucepan covered with a lid over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the tea and stir until dissolved, then let cool to room temperature.
Store the cooled tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days before consumption.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to benign prostatic hypertrophy that are treated with herbal teas are those affecting urinary health and prostate function. Herbal teas like saw palmetto, pygeum, and stinging nettle have been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of enlarged prostate, such as frequent urination and difficulty starting a stream. These teas may also help relieve the symptoms of overactive bladder, interstitial cystitis, and prostatitis by reducing inflammation and promoting urinary tract relaxation.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for benign prostatic hypertrophy are plant-based remedies that help alleviate symptoms of this non-cancerous enlargement of the prostate gland, improving urinary flow and reducing discomfort. The best herbal teas for this condition include Serenoa repens, Urtica dioica, Curcuma longa, Prunus africana, Pygeum africanum, Lycopodium clavatum, Zingiber officinale, Arctostaphylos uva-ursi, Epilobium parviflorum, and Ruscus aculeatus. If left untreated, benign prostatic hypertrophy can lead to urinary frequency, painful urination, and incontinence, significantly impacting a person's quality of life, causing stress, and disrupting daily activities.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with benign prostatic hypertrophy is a blend of saw palmetto, nettle root, and pygeum. Saw palmetto is known to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that contributes to prostate enlargement. Nettle root helps to reduce inflammation and improve urine flow, while pygeum promotes prostate health and reduces symptoms. Drinking a combination of these teas may help alleviate symptoms associated with benign prostatic hypertrophy, such as urinary frequency and difficulty.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Journal of the Indian Medical Association, a 12-week treatment with Prostina, a multi-ingredient herbal formulation, significantly reduced the American Urological Association (AUA) symptom score from 19.50 ± 1.40 to 1.04 ± 0.68, providing symptomatic relief in patients with benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH). The study, which compared Prostina with terazosin, found that the AUA symptom score in the Prostina group was significantly lower than in the terazosin group after 12 weeks (p = 0.005). This suggests that herbal teas containing similar ingredients to Prostina may be a good alternative for managing BPH symptoms, as they can provide effective symptomatic relief comparable to traditional medications like terazosin.
